title: SUNCO EVENT-SPEC SPEC-BLOCK
description: Schema for the YAML SPEC-BLOCK fenced inside EVENT-SPEC.md produced by backend-phase-event (Phase 46/M3.5). Per spec R2 (SUNCO:SPEC-BLOCK deterministic extraction) and BS1 (schema versioning). Lenient additive: listed fields required, additionalProperties permitted so downstream phases (Phase 47 backend-review-event) may extend without schema bump.

version.description: Schema version per BS1. Constant 1 for Phase 46 output. Future schema bumps increment this and ship coordinated schema migrations.

events.description: Event inventory. At least one event per eventing surface.

schema.description: Event payload body schema. Inline TS-like type, JSON Schema ref, or prose. Describes payload only — NOT HTTP request/response (that lives in API-SPEC).

ordering.description: Ordering guarantee for this event family.

delivery_guarantee.description: Delivery semantics for this event family. at-least-once is the distributed-system default; exactly-once requires transactional outbox or idempotent consumers.

retention.description: Retention window for undelivered/replayable events. Accepts duration strings (e.g., '7d', 'forever') or null when not applicable.

dead_letter_strategy.description: Dead-letter queue destination + retry/reprocess posture. Object form preferred (destination + max_retries + backoff + reprocess); string form acceptable when the strategy fits a single sentence.

idempotency_keys.description: Idempotency key provenance + TTL + dedup scope. Object form preferred; string/array acceptable when the strategy is simple.

anti_pattern_watchlist.description: Phase 42 backend-excellence anti-patterns this event surface must avoid. Done-when (Phase 46) requires at least 3 entries, each citing a Phase 42 reference file (e.g., reliability-and-failure-modes.md / boundaries-and-architecture.md). Note: spec §7 Phase 3.6 states 'no deterministic rules v1' for the event surface — entries here are heuristic or human-review candidates.
